subject: docs(claude): align sun-sea-o CLAUDE.md with glow-props — six drift fixes
body:
Ran the `align` trigger against glow-props' CLAUDE.md. Six gaps closed:

Communication section:
- Added "Work, not process" rule: never opine on branching / PRs /
  commit granularity / review flow unless the user raises it. The
  user's domain, not the AI's.

Code Standards section:
- Added "Timer and Subscription Cleanup" subsection covering
  setTimeout/setInterval/addEventListener/subscribe cleanup, HMR guard
  convention, React useEffect return vs plain-module dispose, with a
  pointer to glow-props' TIMER_LEAKS pattern. Cites the existing
  src/utils/debugLog.ts __debugLogListenersAttached guard as the
  in-repo example.

Documentation section:
- Added README.md entry listing purpose / when-to-read / when-to-update
  / content — parity with glow-props and with every other AI-maintained
  doc already listed.

AI Notes section:
- Added "All code is yours" rule: don't hedge authorship, don't treat
  unfamiliar diffs as someone else's work to investigate.
- Added discontinued-repos skip list (plant-fur, coin-zapp) relevant to
  fleet-wide align / pattern-audit runs.

Prohibitions section — added four process-meddling rules:
- "Remove features during 'cleanup'" check (with AI_MISTAKES.md
  cross-ref).
- No mentioning branches / PRs / squashing / rebasing / merging /
  force-pushing unless the user raises it.
- "No out-of-scope framing." Work is doable (do it) or blocked on user
  input (say what's needed). Scope is not a reason to skip.
- No opinions on git history editing / branch strategy / PR size / PR
  shape / commit structure.

Repo-specific divergences (useCallback OMIT default, one-environment
policy, Adopted Patterns catalog, backend API rule, USER_GUIDE +
TESTING_GUIDE docs) intentionally NOT propagated to glow-props —
they're sun-sea-o-specific learnings.

Tags: docs, alignment, claude-md, conventions
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: custom-code-reduction
Semver: patch
